rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Continued Hearing Re Defendants Fourth Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO, CELIA MORENO, M.D. Continued Hearing Re Defendants Fourth Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions. The code requires that plaintiff make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information to answer discovery. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under [her] control." Plaintiff must also make a "diligent search" and a "reasonable inquiry" in responding to a document request. C.C.P. 2031.230 Plaintiff has had two years to properly respond to the discovery. Yet, despite the numerous opportunities to respond, she has violated three separate orders. Therefore, defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is granted. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is granted. Plaintiff to pay defendant County of San Mateo, Celia Moreno, M.D. and their attorneys of record Howard, Rome, Martin & Ridley $1,600.00 plus $60 in costs for a total of $1660.00 no later than May 1, 2015. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK A. BROWN VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Enforcement Of Writ Of Administrative Mandate And Related Relief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2015, Line 2, PETITIONER MARK BROWN Amended Motion For Enforcement Of Writ Of Administrative Mandate And Related Relief. Granted in part and denied in part. Respondents did not reasonably comply with the Court's May 23, 2013 Order and Writ of Administrative Mandamus. The Order and Writ required Respondents to "immediately" pay Petitioner his past-due unemployment benefits and to meet and confer with Petitioner's counsel about the method of payment. It was not reasonably compliant with the Order and Writ to instead unilaterally demand Petitioner retroactively fill out bi-weekly certifications for the entire period before Respondents paid him the past-due benefits, or to refuse to actually meet and confer with Petitioner's attorney as the order required. The Court finds this failure to comply was without good cause or substantial justification, and therefore levies a $1000 fine against Respondents, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1097. However, Respondents are correct that the interest due on the benefits should have been paid at 7% per annum, not 10% as Petitioner alleges. Unemployment benefits are not a contractual obligation. (See Rivera v. Patino (N. D. Cal 1981) 524 F.Supp. 136, 143-44.) As such, the generic interest rate on judgments applies, rather than the 10% rate for breach of contract; Respondents overpaid, rather than underpaid, interest due to Petitioner. Petitioner is entitled to reasonable attorney's fees under Government Code section 800 according to proof. Respondent shall immediately pay any unpaid amount to Petitioner less overpayments, if any. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EVON BELL VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses And Documents To Defendants Request For Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday April 14, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT JOHN BEATTIE'S Motion To Compel Responses And Documents To Defendants Request For Production. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel document response and production of documents is granted. No opposition filed. The response and related documents shall be produced within twenty days after service of this Order with Notice of Entry. Sanctions in the sum of $750.00 are awarded to the moving party as against plaintiff's counsel only. Sanctions to be paid within twenty days of service of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE FINLEY VS. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT DREXEL BRADSHAW DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court overrules the demurrer to the conversion cause of action. Paragraph 34 of the amended complaint alleges that defendant converted plaintiff's money and the demurrer does not address that theory of liability. Plaintiff is correct that defendant cannot demurrer to part of a cause of action. The court sustains the demurrer to the negligence cause of action to give plaintiff an opportunity to comply with this court's 6/6/14 ruling. The court previously ordered plaintiff to allege the date the representation ended and plaintiff admits that she failed to do so through an oversight. The court rejects defendant's reliance on Lazy Acres Market, Inc. v. Tseng (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 1431. In Lazy Acres, the court dismissed a malpractice cause of action where the client alleged that the attorney failed to advise it of a potential or actual conflict of interest. The court held that there were no facts regarding how that breach caused damage. Here, plaintiff alleges damages. Plaintiff claims that defendant billed her for work that was not done and for work that was adverse to plaintiff. (Amended Complaint Par. 14). In addition, a factual issue remains regarding whether plaintiff would have obtained a better outcome but for defendant's alleged negligence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ANITA COEN VS. GENERAL NUTRITION CENTERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT GENERAL NUTRITION CENTERS, INC., DARIN VILHAUER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Adjudication Of Issues. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant has maintained its summary judgment burden on the discrimination/wrongful termination and intentional infliction of emotional distress causes of action. Defendant's separate statement of undisputed facts (material facts 10 - 43) and the declaration of Mr. Vilhauer demonstrate that defendant terminated plaintiff because she was not performing her job in a satisfactory manner. Plaintiff does not dispute most, if not all, of the facts that show that she was not properly performing her job. Thus, the burden has shifted to plaintiff to show pretext. "[T]o avoid summary judgment, an employee claiming discrimination must offer substantial evidence that the employer's stated non-discriminatory reason for the adverse action was untrue or pretextual, or evidence the employer acted with a discriminatory animus, or a combination of the two, such that a reasonable trier of fact could conclude the employer engaged in intentional discrimination." Hersant v. Department of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1004-1005. Here, plaintiff has not met that burden. First, plaintiff's opposing separate statement of material facts does not substantially comply with CRC 3.1350. Second, plaintiff does not present competent evidence showing pretext. Plaintiff argues that Mr. Vilhauer told Troy Dwaine not to help plaintiff. Plaintiff relies on hearsay to prove that assertion, however. (Plaintiff relies on her deposition and the Murray dec. and the court sustains defendant's objections to this evidence based on lack of foundation and hearsay). Plaintiff similarly argues that Mr. Vilhauer targeted other seasoned managers for termination and sought to replace them with young male managers. Plaintiff relies on pgs. 86-88 of her deposition and the Zakkak dec. to support that assertion. That testimony lacks foundation and is based on hearsay and the court also sustains defendant's objections to this evidence. Plaintiff cannot establish the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because the discrimination/wrongful termination cause of action fails. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing ven if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BURST MEDIA CORPORATION VS. IGNITIONADSONLINE, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT GERON COLEMAN Motion To Dismiss. The Motion to Dismiss is denied. Defendant made a general appearance when he sought affirmative relief to compel arbitration. [See Factor Health Mgmt., LLC v. Sup.Ct. (Apex Therapeutic Care, Inc.) (2005) 132 CA4th 246, 250.] The essential elements to be pleaded in an action for breach of contract are: (1) the contract; (2) plaintiff's performance of the contract or excuse for nonperformance; (3) defendants' breach; and (4) the resulting damage to plaintiff. [See Lortz v. Connell, (1969) 273 Cal. App. 2d 286, 290.] In the case at bar Plaintiff has pled each element. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Additional tangential issues shall be establishment of a confidentiality protective order, whether the parties would benefit from the appointment of a Discovery Referee, and should the action be referred to a Single Assignment Department. A second hearing date to rule on issues and objections the parties are unable to resolve will be set. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KING SECURITY SERVICES, INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO CULTURAL ARTS TRADITIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default And And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT JAMES SOWERS Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default And And Default Judgment. The motion is denied. Defendant's motion does not comply with CCP 1008(a). Defendant does not provide a declaration in compliance with the statute. Defendant fails to highlight any new facts, circumstances, or law. Lastly, defendant fails to explain why his "new" evidence could not have been presented earlier. "To be entitled to reconsideration, a party should show that (1) evidence of new or different facts exist, and (2) the party has a satisfactory explanation for failing to produce such evidence at an earlier time." Kalivas v. Barry Controls Corp., (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1152, 1160-1161; see also Gilberd v. AC Transit, (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK HOOSHMAND VS. KATHARINA WOHL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Wohls Attendance At Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday April 14, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MARK HOOSHMAND'S Motion To Compel Defendant Wohls Attendance At Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel the plaintiff to appear for oral deposition is denied without prejudice to be refiled, if necessary, after the Court makes its determination regarding plaintiff's demurrer set for hearing on September 22, 2015. The Court's decision on the demurrer has the potential to be dispositive of the entire lawsuit. The Court has authority to order discovery while pleadings are under attack. However the moving party has not made a sufficient showing of necessity for a deposition to proceed at this time and the debt which is the subject of the action may have been extinguished by the defendant's Chapter 7 bankruptcy. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
